beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Saturday morning wake-up with full energy; Shaan starts the day fresh and ready for weekend activities.

beats.activity: Walk to a nearby café for breakfast and espresso.
beats.reasoning: Sunny Saturday morning is perfect for a neighborhood café walk; establishes a weekend routine before the 11am FIFA match.

beats.activity: Head home to prepare for the USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match.
beats.reasoning: Travel back home to settle in before the 11am kickoff; Light Rail closure doesn't affect walking/transit routes to home.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup group stage match on TV.
beats.reasoning: Shaan is a software engineer who enjoys soccer; the 11am kickoff is a natural focal point for a Saturday, and streaming at home avoids crowds.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat lunch at home.
beats.reasoning: Post-match lunch while the energy is still up; convenient to stay home on a sunny day with no urgent errands.

beats.activity: Walk to a local grocery store to pick up snacks and weekend provisions.
beats.reasoning: Quick neighborhood errand on a sunny afternoon; Light Rail closure is not relevant to local walking routes in Ballard.
Short walk back to home; completes the errand loop before the afternoon wind-down.

beats.activity: Head to Gas Works Park for an outdoor walk and to enjoy the sunny weather.
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny Saturday afternoon for outdoor recreation; park offers views and a refreshing break from home; Light Rail closure doesn't affect driving or biking there.

beats.activity: Commute back home from Gas Works Park.
beats.reasoning: Return journey across town; allows time to rest before evening.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner, then relax with a show or music.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down with dinner and relaxation; Shaan settles in for the night at home before sleep.

diary: Started the day with my usual routine—coffee at home, then walked over to Ballard Coffee Company for a proper espresso and breakfast. The morning was crisp and perfect. Came back home around nine to set up for the USA vs Wales match at eleven. I was pretty hyped about it, honestly. Grabbed some lunch after the game wrapped up, then wandered down to Safeway for snacks and groceries. The weather was too good to waste indoors, so I headed to Gas Works Park in the afternoon. Spent a couple hours just walking around, taking in the sun and the view of the city from up there. It's one of my favorite spots when the weather cooperates like this. By evening I was pretty content—made dinner at home, threw on some music, and wound down. It was one of those days that felt genuinely good, you know?
thought_process: The World Cup match was the anchor of my day, so I structured everything around that. I wanted to be home and settled by eleven, which meant keeping the morning low-key and close by. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me much since I'm usually just walking around Ballard anyway. After the match, I felt restless—sitting inside all morning left me wanting fresh air and movement. Gas Works Park was a natural choice given how beautiful it was out. I think the sunny weather just shifted my whole vibe from 'get through Monday' to actually enjoying it. The grocery run was practical, but the park walk felt like a real break. By the time I got home for dinner, I was satisfied, tired in a good way.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
